topic: homesickness — missing Carnival season in São Paulo

turns.content: You know what I was thinking about today? Carnival. I know it's July and Carnival was months ago but someone posted a video from this year's blocos in São Paulo and I just... saudade hit me like a truck.
That kind of saudade can sneak up on you — one video or song and suddenly you're transported back. What was in the video that got to you?

It was a bloco going through the streets in Vila Madalena, hundreds of people dancing, confetti everywhere, someone playing trumpet on top of a truck. And everyone just — HAPPY. That specific kind of Brazilian happiness where nobody cares how they look, nobody is performing for Instagram, people are just sweating and laughing and dancing together. You can't replicate that energy anywhere else in the world. I started crying at my desk haha, how embarrassing.

That's not embarrassing at all — it sounds like the video captured something that goes deeper than just a party. It's a whole cultural experience tied to identity, community, and belonging. How long has it been since you actually experienced Carnival in person?

Three years!! Three Carnivals I've missed. And every year I say 'next year I'll go back for it' and then work gets busy, flights are expensive during that period, and it doesn't happen. My friends send me videos and photos and voice notes from the blocos and I just sit here in Dubai watching them on my phone feeling like the saddest person alive haha. Ai meu Deus.

Three years is a long stretch to miss something that important to you. It sounds like it's become one of those things that keeps getting pushed to 'someday.' Is there anything specific that would make it possible next year — like booking the flights early before prices spike, or requesting the time off well in advance?

You're right, I need to just commit and book it. Carnival is in February, so if I book flights by October the prices are still reasonable. And I have vacation days — I just never use them for this because I always think 'oh I should save them for something more practical.' But gente — what is more practical than my SOUL?? My soul needs Carnival!! My soul needs to be in the street at 3pm covered in glitter singing Ivete Sangalo at the top of my lungs!

When you put it that way, it does sound pretty essential. Mental health and cultural connection aren't luxuries — they're maintenance. October is a reasonable deadline for booking. Maybe even set a calendar reminder so it doesn't slip past you again. What would the trip look like — just Carnival weekend, or would you extend it?

Oh I would definitely extend it! If I'm flying all the way to Brazil I'm staying at least ten days. Carnival itself is about five days of madness, but I need time before to see my family and eat my mom's food and just BE there, you know? Walk through my old neighborhood, get a pastel de feira, drink a suco de maracuja at that bar that hasn't changed in twenty years. Nossa, just talking about it makes my chest tight.

That tightness is the saudade doing its thing — it's homesickness mixed with love mixed with longing for a version of life that still exists but you're not in it right now. The ten-day plan sounds perfect though. Carnival for the energy, and the days around it for the quieter connections — family, familiar places, comfort food. Those quieter moments might fill your cup even more than the blocos.

You're so right, querida. The quiet moments are what I miss most honestly. Like sitting in the kitchen while my mom cooks and just talking about nothing. Or walking to the padaria in the morning for fresh bread and coffee. Those tiny everyday Brazilian things that you don't even appreciate when you live there because they're just... life. And then you move away and realize — oh, that WAS the good stuff. That was everything.

There's a real wisdom in that — you often can't see the value of the ordinary until distance makes it extraordinary. The kitchen conversations, the morning bakery run, those are the things that make a place feel like home rather than just a location. It sounds like this trip would be less about Carnival and more about reconnecting with all of that.

Both! I need the chaos AND the calm haha. That's what Brazil is — it's both at the same time. Loud and gentle. Messy and beautiful. You know what, I'm making myself emotional again but in a good way. I think I needed this — to actually let myself feel the saudade instead of pushing it down and pretending I'm fine.

Letting yourself feel it is healthier than suppressing it. Saudade isn't a problem to solve — it's evidence of how deeply you're connected to a place and the people in it. That's a good thing, even when it aches. The key is making sure it motivates action rather than just sadness. And it sounds like you're moving toward action.

I am! I'm putting it in my calendar RIGHT NOW — October 1st, book Carnival flights. I'm going. This is happening. Dubai will survive without me for ten days! And if anyone at work has a problem with it, I'll just say it's essential for my mental health and cross-cultural wellbeing haha. Which is actually true!

Good — put it in the calendar and treat it as non-negotiable. Dubai will absolutely survive, and you'll come back recharged in ways that no amount of weekend rest here can replicate. I hope the October reminder holds and that next February you're exactly where you need to be — covered in glitter, singing at the top of your lungs.
